title: Fideo Seco Dry Mexican Noodles

seo.title: Fideo Seco Recipe: Authentic Dry Mexican Noodles
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Fideo Seco, a delicious dry Mexican noodle dish. This easy recipe brings the flavors of Mexico to your kitchen. Perfect for any meal!
seo.h1: Authentic Fideo Seco: Dry Mexican Noodles Recipe
h2Headings: Ingredients You'll Need for Fideo Seco
How to Make Fideo Seco (Step-by-Step Guide)
Tips for the Best Fideo Seco
Variations and Substitutions for Fideo Seco
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of Fideo Seco, traditional dry Mexican noodles, garnished with cilantro and queso fresco.
seo.imageCaption: Savor the rich flavors of homemade Fideo Seco, a classic dry Mexican noodle dish perfect for family dinners.

description: Fideo Seco is a cherished Mexican comfort food, featuring slender fideo noodles simmered in a rich, flavorful tomato-based sauce until all the liquid is absorbed. This dry pasta dish is a staple in Mexican homes, offering a rustic yet deeply satisfying experience that's perfect as a side or a light main course. Its simplicity and robust taste make it an instant family favorite.

instructions.title: Prepare the Sauce Base
instructions.text: In a blender, combine the roughly chopped Roma tomatoes, 1/4 medium white onion, and peeled garlic cloves with 1/2 cup of chicken broth. Blend until the mixture is very smooth, about 1-2 minutes. Set this tomato sauce base aside.

instructions.title: Toast the Fideo Noodles
instructions.text: Heat the vegetable oil in a large skillet or cazuela over medium heat. Add the fideo noodles and toast them, stirring constantly, until they turn a beautiful golden brown. This step is crucial for flavor and texture, so be careful not to burn them. This usually takes 3-5 minutes.

instructions.title: Cook the Tomato Sauce
instructions.text: Carefully pour the blended tomato mixture through a fine-mesh sieve directly into the skillet with the toasted fideo. Press down on any solids in the sieve with a spoon to extract all the liquid, then discard the remaining pulp. Bring the sauce to a gentle simmer, stirring occasionally, for about 5 minutes until it thickens slightly and deepens in color.

instructions.title: Add Broth and Season
instructions.text: Pour in the remaining 1 1/2 cups of chicken broth and add the salt to the skillet. Stir everything well to combine the toasted noodles with the flavorful sauce. Ensure the noodles are submerged in the liquid.

instructions.title: Simmer Until Dry
instructions.text: Bring the mixture to a boil, then immediately reduce the heat to low. Cover the skillet tightly and let it simmer for 15-20 minutes, or until the fideo noodles have absorbed all the liquid and are tender. Check periodically to ensure it does not stick to the bottom of the pan. If needed, add a splash more broth or water.

instructions.title: Rest and Serve
instructions.text: Once the liquid is fully absorbed and the noodles are tender, remove the skillet from the heat and let it rest, still covered, for 5 minutes. This resting period allows the flavors to meld further and the noodles to achieve their perfect texture. Fluff the Fideo Seco gently with a fork before serving.

instructions.title: Garnish and Enjoy
instructions.text: Serve the Fideo Seco hot. Garnish each serving generously with crumbled queso fresco and chopped fresh cilantro. For an extra touch of creaminess, add a dollop of Mexican crema or sour cream if desired. Enjoy this traditional Mexican dry noodle dish!

tips: For extra depth of flavor, roast the tomatoes, onion, and garlic before blending them for the sauce. This adds a smoky, sweeter note.
If you prefer a spicier Fideo Seco, add a small dried chile de arbol or a pinch of chipotle powder to the blender with the tomato mixture.

Fideo Seco is excellent as a side dish to grilled meats like carne asada or chicken, or served simply with a fried egg on top for a light meal. Leftovers can be stored in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
article: Fideo Seco Dry Mexican Noodles is a beloved Mexican Pasta and Noodles dish enjoyed across Mexican. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
